consumers.current_behavior: SQLModel table attests a proposal_id with a human decision (approve/reject/refer). No proposal_version_id, proposal_content_hash, decision_case_version_id, or scenario_version_id. Binds only to proposal_id.
consumers.authority_effect: Defines the database shape; carries no execution authority (authority_level=needs_human_confirm).
prerequisites: Migration path for existing attestation rows is documented.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation table for historical data and receipt replay. New writes that need version-bound decision semantics must use a separate DecisionRecord/DecisionValidity model.

consumers.current_behavior: ReviewEvent carries an optional attestation_ref field linking review events (annotation, archive, reopen, compare_mark) back to the attestation they were created under. The docstring explicitly says attestation stays the decision of record.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only link; ReviewEvent never carries execution authority.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation_ref in ReviewEvent as a historical compatibility link. No migration needed.

consumers.current_behavior: Docstring claims: 'attestation stays the decision of record (approve/reject); ReviewEvent records annotation / archive / reopen / compare_mark / agent_review_note / agent_scaffold_revision_apply_candidate.'
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation only; does not change behavior.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace 'attestation stays the decision of record' with 'Attestation is the historical human review decision. Canonical, version-bound decisions will be captured in DecisionRecord.'

consumers.current_behavior: The Decision enum (approved/rejected/referred) is used by Attestation.decision. It represents a human review outcome, not a version-bound canonical decision.
consumers.authority_effect: Typing only; no authority effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Decision enum for historical Attestation rows. A separate enum may be needed for DecisionRecord.

consumers.current_behavior: Attestation is included in the StateCoreRecord union used by write_records and read-back typing.
consumers.authority_effect: Enables write_records persistence path for Attestation.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation in StateCoreRecord for existing data. When DecisionRecord is added, it will also be added to StateCoreRecord.

consumers.current_behavior: Creates Attestation row + receipt file + ReceiptIndex. Records proposal_receipt_ref in receipt payload but the Attestation row only binds proposal_id (no version). Includes P5 forcing gate for high-risk proposals.
consumers.authority_effect: Writes an Attestation row and receipt. Does not authorize execution.
prerequisites: Version-bound decision write surface exists on main.
Existing attestation migration path is documented.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep create_governed_attestation operational for existing consumers. Add a separate create_decision_record command that writes version-bound decisions. Deprecate create_governed_attestation once all consumers migrate.
test_implications: tests/test_agent_proposal_drafts.py tests attestation existence as review completion.

consumers.current_behavior: Return type of create_governed_attestation. Carries attestation_id, proposal_id, receipt_ref, and ReceiptIndex.
consumers.authority_effect: Return value only; no authority effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep for attestation receipt compatibility. Future DecisionRecord writes will have their own return type.

consumers.current_behavior: RecordReviewEvent payload includes an optional attestation_ref. When a review event is written under an existing attestation, this field links them.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only link; the review event itself carries no execution authority.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation_ref in RecordReviewEvent for historical linkage.

consumers.current_behavior: POST /proposals/{proposal_id}/attest endpoint. Calls create_governed_attestation. Returns AttestationCreateResponse with attestation receipt. Tagged 'review' and not 'legacy'.
consumers.authority_effect: Deprecated on the legacy surface; the primary attest endpoint is still active. Does not authorize execution.
prerequisites: Attest endpoint can be flagged as legacy/deprecated.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep /attest endpoint for existing consumers (frontend attestation form). When DecisionRecord endpoint exists, tag this as legacy and redirect consumers.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets open_for_review = not attestations. A proposal is considered 'open for review' when it has zero attestations. Uses attestation existence as a binary review-completion gate.
consumers.authority_effect: Changes proposal review lifecycle state in API responses. Does not authorize execution.
prerequisites: Dual-read path exists for transition period.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace open_for_review = not attestations with a query against DecisionValidity. During transition, use dual read: check DecisionValidity first, fall back to Attestation.
test_implications: tests/test_statecore_api.py tests proposal review response status.

consumers.current_behavior: API response model exposes open_for_review: bool derived from attestation existence. Consumers (frontend, tests) use this to decide whether proposal needs review.
consumers.authority_effect: API shape contract; affects all consumers of GET /proposals/{id}.
consumers.recommended_change: Add decision_validity field to ProposalReviewResponse. During transition, set open_for_review from both sources. Eventually deprecate attestation-only derivation.

consumers.current_behavior: ReviewTaskLifecycle has state 'completed' derived from attestation presence. open_for_review field in lifecycle also uses attestation absence/presence.
consumers.authority_effect: Drives review task UX; does not authorize execution.
consumers.recommended_change: Derive ReviewTaskLifecycle.state from DecisionValidity instead of Attestation. During transition, both sources contribute.

consumers.current_behavior: GET /proposals supports status=open|attested|all filter. status=open filters to proposals with open_for_review=True. status=attested filters to open_for_review=False. Uses attestation existence for filtering.
consumers.authority_effect: API filtering contract.
consumers.recommended_change: During transition, include decision_validity_status in filtering logic. Keep 'open'/'attested' labels for backward compatibility.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets review_state = 'human_attestation_recorded' when open_for_review is False (attestation exists). Sets 'pending_human_review' when open_for_review is True.
consumers.authority_effect: Agent review surface state — affects agent context presentation.
consumers.recommended_change: Derive AgentReviewSurface.review_state from DecisionValidity instead of attestation presence.

consumers.current_behavior: Computes count of open proposals by filtering out those with attestations. Uses attestation existence to determine proposal openness for cockpit dashboard.
consumers.authority_effect: Dashboard display only; read-only projection.
consumers.recommended_change: During transition, count open proposals from DecisionValidity instead of Attestation. Fall back to attestation count for backward compatibility.

consumers.current_behavior: Queries all Attestation rows and builds attested_ids set. Each proposal in attested_ids gets queue status 'reviewed'. This drives the review queue UX.
consumers.authority_effect: Drives review queue priority and status; does not authorize execution.
prerequisites: All review queue consumers are identified.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace attested_ids lookup with DecisionValidity.status query. During transition, merge both sources.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets review queue status to 'reviewed' when proposal_id is in attested_ids. This is the core queue classification logic.
consumers.authority_effect: Directly classifies proposals in the review queue.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace attested_ids membership check with DecisionValidity status check.

consumers.current_behavior: Merges Attestation and ReviewEvent into a unified timeline. Each Attestation is presented as a timeline entry with source_type='attestation'. This is historical evidence display.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only display; historical evidence preservation.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation in timeline forever as historical review evidence. When DecisionRecord exists, add it as a separate timeline entry type.

consumers.current_behavior: Sets open_for_review = proposal_id not in attested_ids. Computes review_state fields from attestation presence.
consumers.authority_effect: Per-proposal queue item classification.
consumers.recommended_change: Derive open_for_review from DecisionValidity. During transition, combine both sources.

consumers.current_behavior: Uses attested_ids to exclude already-attested proposals from duplicate detection. Only considers non-attested proposals as potential duplicates.
consumers.authority_effect: Affects duplicate proposal detection and review queue display.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace attested_ids filter with DecisionValidity status check.

consumers.current_behavior: Lists 'human_attestation' as a blocked transition for proposals that are not ready. Treats attestation as a transition gate.
consumers.authority_effect: Gate naming; does not change attestation behavior directly.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep the transition name 'human_attestation' for backward compatibility. Future blocked transitions may use 'decision_record' instead.

consumers.current_behavior: non_claims tuple includes: 'Human review is required before any decision of record.' Implicitly equates attestation with decision of record.
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation claim; no direct authority effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Rephrase to: 'Human review produces a historical attestation. Canonical, version-bound decisions are captured in DecisionRecord.'

consumers.current_behavior: Docstring: 'Attestation stays the decision of record; review events are the interaction ledger.'
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation claim; no direct authority effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace with: 'Attestation is historical review evidence. DecisionRecord captures version-bound canonical decisions.'

consumers.current_behavior: Two AgentToolEntry definitions include non_claims: 'Human review is required before any decision of record.' Implicitly treats attestation as decision of record.
consumers.authority_effect: Documentation claim in agent tool metadata.
consumers.recommended_change: Rephrase agent tool non_claims to distinguish historical attestation from version-bound decision.

consumers.current_behavior: States: 'invariants: append-only; attestation is decision of record, not execution.'
consumers.authority_effect: Architecture documentation claim.
prerequisites: System map updated with DecisionRecord entry.
consumers.recommended_change: Replace with: 'Attestation is historical human review evidence. DecisionRecord is the version-bound canonical decision.'

consumers.current_behavior: Renders attestation list on proposal detail page. Displays decision, attester, reason for each attestation. Pure display of historical data.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only display. No authority effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation display as historical evidence. When DecisionRecord exists, add a separate display section for canonical decisions.

consumers.current_behavior: Renders attestation form (decision select, attester, reason) and POSTs to /proposals/{id}/attest. This is the active write surface.
consumers.authority_effect: User-facing write surface; does not authorize execution.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation form for backward compatibility. Add a separate DecisionRecord form when available. Eventually switch default form to DecisionRecord.

consumers.current_behavior: Renders merged timeline showing attestations alongside review events. Each attestation entry shows source_type='attestation', kind, attester. Read-only historical display.
consumers.authority_effect: Read-only display; historical evidence.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation timeline entries as historical evidence. When DecisionRecord exists, add it as a separate timeline entry type.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests attestation creation, read, and response shape. Locks current attestation API contract including open_for_review computation and status filtering.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: When DecisionRecord exists, add dual-read transition tests that verify both attestation and DecisionRecord paths. Existing attestation tests must continue to pass for backward compatibility.
test_implications: Full attestation API contract test file.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests that agent proposal drafts interact correctly with attestation lifecycle. Verifies attestation presence affects review state.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: When DecisionRecord exists, extend tests to cover dual-read path. Keep existing attestation lifecycle tests.
test_implications: Full agent proposal attestation test file.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests Attestation model persistence, serialization round-trips, and StateCore reconstruction. Locks current Attestation schema.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep Attestation serialization tests for historical data integrity. Add DecisionRecord serialization tests separately.

consumers.current_behavior: Tests risk classification related to attestation decisions. Uses attestation decision state in risk evaluation.
consumers.authority_effect: Test contract only.
consumers.recommended_change: Review whether risk classification should use DecisionRecord instead of Attestation. Transition after DecisionRecord exists.

consumers.current_behavior: Registers attestation as a receipt-backed write in the governance write registry. Documents the attestation write surface for governance auditing.
consumers.authority_effect: Governance documentation; no runtime effect.
consumers.recommended_change: Keep attestation entry in write registry. When DecisionRecord write exists, add a separate entry.

exclusions.reason: Only uses attestation_ref as a context summary key for agent context pack building. This is derived data processing, not a direct Attestation consumer.
Uses attestation_ref as a context source for scaffold candidate preflight checks. Indirect reference through context data, not a direct Attestation consumer.
Uses status='attested' for DomainMemory entries. This is an unrelated domain (memory, not proposal attestation). Same word, different concept.
Mentions Attestation in context of daily brief data aggregation. Reads Attestation as part of general proposal state collection. No decision semantics attached.
Reads Attestation as part of annual review period summary. Collects historical attestation data for review. No current-state gate semantics.
Includes Attestation in StateCoreRecord union type for write_records. This is a persistence infrastructure concern, not a semantic consumer.
Re-exports Attestation model. Module-level import; not a consumer.
Lists Attestation as an Object artifact in the abstraction taxonomy. Documentation inventory, not a code consumer.
References Attestation in evolution roadmap as a current capability. Planning document, not a code consumer.
Mentions Attestation in system directory documentation. Documentation standard reference, not a code consumer.
Documents human_attestation as a review lifecycle state for agent runtime. Runtime reference documentation, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in agent runtime skill documentation. Runtime reference documentation, not a code consumer.
Lists Attestation in scaffolding inventory. Documentation inventory, not a code consumer.
Mentions 'human decision of record' in external research context. Research note, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in product thesis context. Product documentation, not a code consumer.
Documents attestation endpoint in API interface reference. API documentation, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in personal finance decision workflow proposal. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in review workspace proposal. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in cockpit lifecycle plan. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in evolution execution plan. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in consolidation proposal. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in StateCore execution brief proposal. Historical proposal document.
Uses 'attester' field name in scaffold revision form. Same field name, different concept (scaffold attester, not Attestation model).
Uses 'attester' field name in review event form. Same field name, different concept (review event attester, not Attestation model).
Uses decisionLabel for attestation display. Already covered by ATT-CONS-026 (renderAttestations).
Shows 'Attested' option in status filter dropdown. Already covered by ATT-CONS-013 (status filter).
CSS class .tag.attested for visual styling. Presentation only, covered by frontend display consumers.
Uses attester field in compare_mark review event tests. Review event testing, not Attestation model testing.
Uses attester field in scaffold revision tests. Scaffold testing, not Attestation model testing.
Mentions attestation in agent runtime reference index. Documentation index, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in industry benchmark analysis. External benchmark document, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in gap register. External benchmark document, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in solution research. External benchmark document, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in product roadmap benchmark. External benchmark document, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in agent harness notes. Implementation notes, not a code consumer.
Mentions attestation in StateCore API proposal. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in debt cleanup plan. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in cockpit retrospective. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in golden path demo. Historical proposal document.
Mentions attestation in SEC market access review. Historical review document.
References Attestation model in policy registry listing. Governance metadata, not a test contract.
Creates Attestation test fixtures. Test helper, not a semantic test contract.
Uses Attestation in agent capabilities test. Infrastructure test, not a direct Attestation contract test.
References Attestation in agent tools test context. Agent tool testing, not Attestation-specific.
Uses Attestation data for annual review period testing. Read-only aggregation test.
Tests receipt-backed write registry that includes attestation. Governance infrastructure test.
Tests review workspace API that includes attestation data. Review workspace testing, not Attestation-specific.
Tests StateCore store that writes Attestation records. Store infrastructure test, not Attestation-specific.
